texto: What is freedom? It consists in two things: to know each his own limitations and accept them – that is the same thing as to know oneself, and accept oneself as one is, without fear, or envy, or distaste; and to recognise and accept the conditions under which one lives, also without fear or envy, or distaste. When you do this, you shall be free.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ann Bridge que provoca enfrentar incertezas com ação. Ao questionar “What is freedom? It consists in two things: to know each his own limitations and accept them – th…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, freedom, happiness, com foco em happiness e self-acceptance.

It consists in two things: to know each his own limitations and accept them – that is the same thing as to know oneself, and accept oneself as one is, without fear, or envy, or distaste; and to recognise and accept the conditions under which one lives, also without fear or envy, or distaste. When you do this, you shall be free.
